name: Planet Labs PBC

profile.description: Planet Labs PBC operates the world's largest commercial Earth observation satellite constellation with 200+ satellites, providing daily global imaging data to governments, agriculture, forestry, defense, and financial intelligence customers. The company reported record Q1 FY2027 revenue of $94M (up 42% YoY) and achieved its first full fiscal year of non-GAAP profitability in FY2026. Planet is pivoting from a satellite hardware company to an AI-powered data-intelligence platform, with on-orbit AI processing now running on Pelican-4 satellites powered by NVIDIA Jetson Orin modules.

innovation.label: STRONG
innovation.analysis: Track Record (3/3): Planet pioneered the small-satellite constellation concept for daily global imaging and has successfully pivoted from satellite manufacturing to a data-intelligence platform — achieving first full-year non-GAAP profitability in FY2026 ($15.5M adjusted EBITDA) while growing revenue 26% to $307.7M [1]. In April 2026, Planet achieved a landmark technical first: running AI-driven object detection directly onboard its Pelican-4 satellite, enabling on-orbit compute for near-real-time insights — a capability Planet calls 'Planetary Intelligence' [2]. AI Deployment (2/2): Planet is shipping AI-powered analytics products (crop health monitoring, deforestation detection, maritime tracking) to enterprise and government customers, with its AI analytics feeds powered by deep learning and computer vision transforming daily imagery into actionable intelligence. The Czech government signed a 7-figure deal specifically for AI-enabled satellite monitoring of 25,000 farms [2]. Market Share (2/2): Planet operates the world's largest commercial Earth observation constellation — category leader in daily global coverage with 200+ satellites [1][2]. Product Pipeline (1/2): FY2027 revenue guidance of $415–$440M (39% growth at midpoint), Q1 FY2027 already at $94M (42% YoY growth), and $906M backlog (72% growth YoY) provide strong visibility. Project Suncatcher joint venture with Google exploring space-based AI data centers [2]. R&D Intensity (0/1): R&D is embedded in operations as a data platform company rather than separately capitalized at above-industry rates.

inspiration.label: DEVELOPING
inspiration.analysis: Mission Floor (3/4): Planet's mission — 'to image the world every day, and make change visible, accessible and actionable' — is specific, service-oriented, and validated by 16 years of consistent execution since founding by three NASA scientists in 2010 [3]. The company's data genuinely serves deforestation monitoring, disaster response, climate research, and national security — a purpose that shapes product decisions. Community Impact (1/2): Planet provides data to NGOs, research organizations, and humanitarian efforts at reduced rates, and its satellite services support Sweden's Armed Forces and Czech agricultural monitoring — genuine community and national security impact [2][3]. Employee Retention (1/2): The 945-person team has been built and maintained through SPAC-era volatility, leadership transitions, and the path to profitability under consistent founder-led management — suggesting mission-driven retention. Insider Buying (1/2): Net insider selling in 2026 — Director Kristen Robinson sold $1.3M, CSO Robert Schingler sold $2.58M, and Director Ita Brennan sold $1.6M — though these are modest relative to total holdings and occurred after significant stock appreciation [3].

infrastructure.label: DEVELOPING
infrastructure.analysis: Growth sleeve stock. Liquidity check: $32.22 x 13.5M avg daily volume = $435M — well above $1M floor. Mechanically scored on available metrics. D/E of 110% scores 0.826 — total debt $488M offset by $731M cash [1]. ROE of -0.84% is negative (score 0.0). Revenue growth YoY of 42.1% is well above the 10% target (score 1.0). 3-year revenue CAGR of 17.2% exceeds the 10% target (score 1.0). Profit margin of -111% is deeply negative from non-cash charges (score 0.0). Forward P/E and PEG excluded (negative earnings). The company generated $84.9M in free cash flow in FY2026, its first year of positive FCF, but GAAP profitability remains elusive. Weighted sum: 3.826 / 7 weight units x 10 = 5.

ai_resilience.label: LOW RISK
ai_resilience.analysis: AI Risk Protection (8/9): Planet Labs cannot be disrupted by AI — it is the proprietary data source that AI earth observation models require. The physical satellite constellation, daily imaging cadence, 16-year historical archive, and government-licensed orbital slots create a data moat that AI structurally cannot replicate without the physical infrastructure [1][2]. AI advancement makes Planet's data more valuable, not less — every new computer vision model needs Planet's imagery as training data. No analyst has flagged AI as a substitution risk; the consensus view is that AI is Planet's primary growth driver. AI Opportunity (1/1): Planet is generating quantifiable AI revenue — AI-powered analytics products (crop monitoring, deforestation alerts, maritime tracking) are a growing share of revenue, the Czech government signed a 7-figure AI satellite monitoring deal, and the company successfully deployed on-orbit AI processing on Pelican-4 satellites [2].

infinite_game.summary: Planet Labs' 16-year mission to image the entire Earth every day is one of the most audacious Just Causes in the space industry. Founded by three NASA scientists who believed satellite data should be accessible to everyone, the company has maintained this mission through a SPAC listing, multiple near-death financial experiences, and the challenge of transitioning from satellite operator to AI-powered data platform. The achievement of FCF profitability while maintaining 42%+ revenue growth validates the long game.

just_cause.analysis: Just Cause (2/3): Planet's purpose — 'to image the world every day, and make change visible, accessible and actionable' — is genuine, service-oriented, and idealistic. Founded by NASA scientists who believed daily Earth observation should be democratized, the mission has been consistently pursued through 16 years of satellite constellation building, SPAC-era volatility, and the pivot to data intelligence [3]. Strategic decisions — building the largest constellation, providing humanitarian data access, pivoting to AI analytics — all trace back to this purpose. However, the mission hasn't yet survived a true leadership transition or existential crisis at scale.

trusting_teams.analysis: Trusting Teams (1/2): Planet's 945-person team has navigated from startup through SPAC to first profitability under consistent founder-led management. The achievement of FCF profitability and 42% revenue growth suggests organizational cohesion and mission alignment. No major cultural controversies or leadership instability on record. However, no specific evidence of exceptional retention metrics or trust-building cultural practices [1].

worthy_rivals.analysis: Worthy Rivals (1/1): Planet explicitly positions against Maxar (defense/high-resolution imaging), Satellogic, BlackSky, and Airbus Defence as competitors whose strengths in high-resolution or SAR imaging push Planet to improve its own revisit rate, analytics capabilities, and on-orbit processing. The competitive language focuses on differentiation and improvement rather than destruction [2][3].

existential_flexibility.analysis: Existential Flexibility (2/2): Planet has undergone a genuine strategic transformation — from satellite hardware manufacturer to AI-powered data intelligence platform. The company recognized that long-term value lies in the analytics layer built on its unique data, not in the satellites themselves. The on-orbit AI deployment on Pelican-4 and Project Suncatcher space-based AI data center JV with Google represent the next evolution of this pivot [2]. This mirrors Fujifilm's pivot from film to materials science.

courage_to_lead.analysis: Courage to Lead (1/2): Maintaining investment in satellite launches and data infrastructure through years of losses to achieve FCF profitability in FY2026 required sustained courage. The on-orbit AI deployment bet and Google Suncatcher JV are forward-looking investments. However, the company has not yet made a truly asymmetric bet — the recent milestones are validation of the original model, not a transformative new wager [1][2].

investment_thesis: Planet Labs is accelerating through a critical inflection — Q1 FY2027 revenue hit a record $94M (up 42% YoY), full-year FY2027 guidance of $415–$440M implies 39% growth, and backlog of $906M (up 72% YoY) provides multi-year revenue visibility [1]. The company achieved its first full-year of non-GAAP profitability in FY2026 ($15.5M adjusted EBITDA, $84.9M free cash flow) and is now deploying AI directly onboard its Pelican-4 satellites — a technical breakthrough that enables near-real-time intelligence from orbit [2]. With the world's largest commercial Earth observation constellation, a growing government customer base (Sweden, Czech Republic, U.S. defense), and the transition to AI-powered analytics generating higher-margin recurring revenue, Planet is becoming the 'Bloomberg of Earth data.'
thesis_continued: The bear case centers on valuation and profitability sustainability. At $32.22 per share, Planet trades at a $11.5B market cap — 27x FY2027 revenue guidance — despite persistent GAAP losses (profit margin -111%) and negative ROE [1]. Insider selling has been notable: CSO Schingler sold $2.58M and directors sold $2.9M combined in early 2026, suggesting insiders may view the stock as fully valued after its run from $4.90 to $32 [3]. Competition from SAR providers (Capella Space, Umbra) offering all-weather imaging and high-resolution providers (Maxar, Airbus) could pressure pricing in government markets. The 200+ satellite constellation requires continuous replenishment launches, and any satellite manufacturing or launch delays could degrade coverage quality.
key_catalysts: FY2027 revenue hitting $415M+ and first full-year GAAP profitability would confirm the AI data-intelligence business model at scale and unlock institutional investors with profitability mandates
On-orbit AI processing expanding from Pelican-4 to the broader constellation, reducing latency from hours to minutes for intelligence customers and commanding premium pricing for real-time analytics
New multi-year sovereign intelligence contracts — Planet's Sweden reconnaissance satellite and Czech farm monitoring deals demonstrate a path toward $100M+ annual government partnerships
key_risks: Valuation at 27x forward revenue with persistent GAAP losses creates vulnerability to any revenue miss or guidance reduction — the stock rose 550% from its 52-week low and insider selling suggests limited margin of safety

Satellite constellation replenishment risk — maintaining 200+ satellites requires continuous launches, and any manufacturing delays, launch vehicle failures, or orbital debris events could degrade daily imaging coverage

Competition from synthetic aperture radar (SAR) providers offering all-weather, day/night imaging capabilities that optical satellites cannot match — increased SAR adoption by defense customers could erode Planet's government market share
sources: Planet Labs Q4 FY2026 and Full-Year FY2026 Earnings Release, March 2026
